references.text: [Va99] Various, Some of Paul's favorite problems. Booklet produced for the conference "Paul Erd\H{o}s and his mathematics", Budapest, July 1999 (1999).

remarks.text:
In \cite{Va99} it is reported that Erd\H{o}s and Selfridge found 'the exact bound' when $2<\alpha<3$, and that 'if $\alpha>3$ then very little is known'. No reference is given, and I cannot find a relevant paper of Erd\H{o}s and Selfridge.

See also [970].

statement.text:
Let $p_1<\cdots<p_u$ be primes and let $k\geq 1$. Let $F_k(p_1,\ldots,p_u)$ be such that every interval of $k$ positive integers contains at least $F_k(p_1,\ldots,p_u)$ multiples of at least one of the $p_i$.

Estimate $F_k(p_1,\ldots,p_u)$, particularly in the range $k=\alpha p_u$ for constant $\alpha>2$.
